headline: 3 S&P 500 Stocks That Concern Us
summary: The S&P 500 (^GSPC) is home to the biggest and most well-known companies in the market, making it a go-to index for investors seeking stability. But not all large-cap stocks are created equal - some are struggling with slowing growth, declining margins, or increased competition.

headline: Carnival Drops 22% This Month As Unhedged Fuel Strategy Backfires
summary: CCL is up 3% as oil pulls back, but still down 22% this month. Carnival doesn't hedge fuel purchases, unlike RCL and NCLH. Oil spike may cut earnings by $0.20. Traders predict low chance of oil falling to $80 or normal shipping in Strait of Hormuz by April 30. Recession chances up to 34%. Carnival to report Q1 earnings on March 20, but unlikely to change fuel strategy. If oil drops, will Carnival lock in cheaper fuel or make same bet again?

headline: Oil Shock, Private Credit Cracks & Adobe's Bittersweet Beat
summary: Brent crude punched through $100 a barrel for the first time since 2022, Iran's new Supreme Leader vowed to keep the Strait of Hormuz closed, and a simmering private credit crisis finally boiled over onto bank stock prices — all in a single Thursday session. Welcome to the new normal.

headline: Madison Square Garden Sports Gains Tabor Backing as Knicks and Rangers Franchise Values Climb
summary: Madison Square Garden Sports owns the New York Knicks and New York Rangers, two of the most valuable franchises in U.S. professional sports. For investors, MSGS's appeal lies in the long-term rise of scarce franchise assets and expanding league media rights as much as in the teams’ yearly operating performance.
